title: ricinus communis subsp. scaber
intro: Ricinus communis subsp. scaber, also known as the scabrous castor oil plant, is a subspecies of the Ricinus communis plant, a flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Ricinus communis subsp. scaber lie in its seeds, which contain ricin, a toxic compound, and ricinoleic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Ricinus communis subsp. scaber include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and its potential to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Ricinus communis subsp. scaber can be prepared as herbal remedies, including ointments, salves, and infusions, which are often used to treat skin issues and promote wound healing.
